excerpt: As an Assistant Manager, you will assist the Profit Center Manager in applying Sunbelt policies. Concentrating focus on maximizing the profitability of the Profit Center ensuring it runs efficiently and effectively. DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES In charge of Profit Center in the absence of the Profit Center Manager. Assess daily the need for purchasing equipment through feedback from salesman, CSRs, customer feedback, vendor representatives and marketing department product managers. Assess and modify (if needed) weekly sales plans and ensure that they are carried out appropriately. Ensure on-going compliance with all statutory DOT and safety requirements. Perform dispatching duties (if needed) Inspect Profit Center yard daily. Ensure OSRs and CSRs are aware of slow moving equipment. Ensure that Shop Foreman is aware of all equipment not available for rent. Various reporting, including, but not limited to: daily contract reports/AP on hold/accrual reports/asset disposal report.

This role will be responsible for all branch functions including rental operations, service and maintenance, logistics, and yard management. Direct and oversee all aspects of day-to-day branch operations, including rental, service, logistics, and yard activities. Partner closely with Sales and Finance leadership to optimize branch performance and ensure seamless communication between operations and corporate functions. Oversee fleet management strategies in coordination with corporate asset and service departments, ensuring maximum equipment utilization and uptime. Monitor and analyze operational KPIs, identifying improvement opportunities and aligning resources accordingly.

Many rental companies are taking advantage of the latest technology to empower their sales staff to be able to access inventory availability through their phones or figure out rates, reserve a machine for their customer, and many more capabilities. Even service technicians can look at service orders, input parts and labor used on a work order and change the work-order status. Their smartphones are becoming tools of their trade as important as any in their toolbox. Or to tell your branch manager in Wichita that utilization on 40-foot boomlifts is 15 percent below average for that city.

Most rental companies have a database that includes the name of your customer, his or her business and their purchasing history. This will help make the interaction more personal and help build that solid relationship. Is this a transactional sale that can be handled by an employee at the counter, or should an outside salesperson respond? Typically, a person with sales experience is better able to develop and maintain a relationship with the customer. You are not finished with a customer once the order has been fulfilled. Call them to make sure they are completely satisfied with what you have provided. Customer service and sales skills do not come naturally to your staff. They need to be trained in these skills, and the skills should be reinforced.

Equipment rental software like RentalMan is designed to manage the end-to-end equipment rental lifecycle-from quoting and fleet tracking to maintenance, invoicing, and reporting. Our equipment rental management software includes features like flexible rental billing, asset maintenance tracking, contract management, and utilization metrics. Our GPS fleet management software gives dispatchers real-time visibility through live GPS Maps, where you and your dispatchers can see real-time truck, equipment, and driver locations right on the scheduler map. RentalMan offers an end-to-end solutions tailored for the equipment rental industry. Unlike generic ERPs or basic rental platforms, our software integrates fleet utilization tools, advanced billing cycles, location-aware logistics - all within one system.

RentalResult is a platform that plans, tracks, bills, and optimizes owned and rented equipment across projects, branches, and yards, tying operations, finance, logistics, and maintenance into one source of truth. Higher utilization, fewer idle days, faster turns, accurate job chargebacks, cleaner month-end close, reduced re-rent spend, and data to inform capex (what to buy, retire, or move). RentalResult exposes idle time by class/branch/job, recommends transfers, highlights chronic underuse, and tracks trends vs. plan so teams act before assets sit. RentalResult does this by forecasting demand, balancing fleet mix, and surfacing internal supply first. It also compares buy vs. rent vs. re-rent to avoid unnecessary vendor spend.

As fleets grow, equipment tends to spread out-across jobsites, warehouses, laydown yards, repair areas, and transfer routes. Without a consistent way to record where equipment is held and what condition it is in, equipment teams run into familiar problems: "We own it, but we can't find it." "It should be available, but it's not ready." "We don't know if it's in the warehouse or still on the job." "We're buying or re-renting because we can't confirm what we have." Instead of piecing together equipment status from multiple places, teams can use item holding to get a clearer view of where equipment is and whether it's available.

If your telematics data flows directly from the machine to your rental software and triggers the creation of a work order to check out an engine that's running too hot and that sends a message to your mechanic on the road who's got his smart phone app synced with your software then your entire business is moving incredibly efficiently. Not so long ago, your yard guys needed to take pieces of paper and clip boards into the yard, write equipment numbers on to say that something has been allocated and then they'd hand those back into an administrator to enter into your computer system. This is no longer the way it has to be! This kind of fluidity, delivering the right information in the right format to people in the yard, in the field or in the office so that they can act on it without waiting for paperwork to be processed, or data re-keyed should be a key part of any current and future business strategy.

If you're running a construction rental business, you know the chaos: A dozer gets double-booked. A backhoe misses service and breaks down. Your team's stuck filling out forms instead of helping customers. These aren't just operational hiccups, they're revenue leaks, lost opportunities, and compounding frustrations that affect your bottom line. With Texada, you can view every asset across every branch from one centralized dashboard. Time is money, and delayed invoices mean delayed profits. Texada's rental management software automates your quoting, contract generation, work order management, and billing processes. "One of the things I love about Texada is any kind of report that I've ever had to put together myself, Texada will spit it out. Texada is worth its weight in gold, to be able to do that."
